methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $2M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
HIMERA is a seed-stage defense and security company founded by Misha Rudominsky and Oleksiy Oliynyk that has raised $2.5M to date. The relevant comp universe includes 2 public peers (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private companies in the defense sector, including later-stage reference points such as Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, Saronic, and Quantum-Systems.

The stage-progression method is the applicable triangulation approach here. Using the $2.5M total raised as a proxy for current value and applying a typical 2.5x multiplier for the Seed-to-next-stage transition yields an implied valuation of $6.25M. This reflects the incremental capital and operational progress expected between seed and Series A in this sector.

Peer-median analysis could not be applied due to insufficient comparable data at the same stage and tier—while 19 companies operate in Defense & Security broadly, the narrow filters of stage, adjacency, and valuation tier yielded fewer than 3 datapoints, which would introduce unwarranted precision. The single-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $5M to $7M, with a median of $6M. Key uncertainties include the company's specific technology maturity, IP position, and customer traction relative to the later-stage peers referenced, all of which would materially influence post-seed fundraising outcomes.
